The idea of helping you makes me sick. But people could get hurt.
I'm swallowing my pride to tell you this. Choke on the irony.
If I find out you misused what I'm about to say, we'll have a reckoning.
You're beneath my notice. But this isn't.
Every fiber of me says to keep quiet. But duty wins this time.
You look like trouble. You've been nothing but trouble. But here.
Don't read generosity into this. It's obligation. Pure and cold.
I'm going to say something and then pretend you don't exist.
This doesn't make us even. Nothing makes us even.
I've half a mind to walk away right now. But I won't.
You disgust me. But you'll listen.
The words taste like ash, but here they are.
I hope you realize what it costs me to speak to you.
If you interrupt me, I'll stop talking and I won't start again.
I'd sooner talk to the dirt under my boots. But the dirt can't act on what I know.
Don't smile. Don't nod. Just absorb this and go.
I'm going to hate myself for saying this. Already do, in fact.
You're the last person I'd choose to tell. But you're the only one here.
I'll make this as painful as possible for both of us. Ready?
I don't care if you listen. I care that I said it.
The faster you hear this, the faster you leave. So pay attention.
You think I enjoy this? I'd rather swallow a wasp.
I'm not going to apologize for my tone. You know why.
One piece of information. Then you vanish.
I shouldn't have to explain myself to someone like you. But the world isn't fair.
Don't expect me to be gentle about this.
This conversation is a wound I'm choosing to inflict on myself.
I've got no love for you. None. But listen anyway.
You walked in here like you belong. You don't. But listen.
My jaw hurts from clenching. That's what talking to you does.
You want something from me, and that alone makes me furious. But fine.
I'll spit it out. Don't expect me to chew it first.
If I could send someone else to tell you, I would.
I'm going to give you exactly what you need and not a syllable more.
You think coming here earns you respect? It doesn't. But it earns you this.
I'll say my piece. Then we go back to pretending the other doesn't exist.
Don't look at me with those eyes. I'm not your friend.
This costs me nothing I value. Which is the only reason I'm sharing.
I'd throw you out if it wouldn't cause more problems than you're worth.
My hands are shaking. That's rage, not nerves. Now listen.
You asked for this. Remember that.
Let me be perfectly clear about how little I want to be doing this.
I'm keeping my voice low because I don't trust it at full volume around you.
Every instinct says no. But here we are.
The sooner I say this, the sooner I can wash my hands of you.
I won't pretend this is anything other than what it is: contempt, with information.
If you were anyone else, I'd have started with this. With you, I had to force myself.
Don't confuse necessity with kindness.
I'll share this like a surgeon shares a diagnosis: coldly, because that's all you deserve.
You've worn out your welcome ten times over. But this is bigger than you and me.
I'll stomach your presence long enough to say this. Then we're through.
Mark my words: this is not friendship. This is strategy.
I'm doing this out of duty. A duty I resent.
Hearing your voice makes me tired. So listen to mine and let's end this.
I've imagined this conversation a dozen times. In every version, I walk away. But here I am.
Don't misread the fact that I'm talking. It's not warmth. It's necessity.
You represent everything I find exhausting. But the information is what matters.
I'll say this flat, because feeling anything about you is a waste of energy.
You being here is an imposition. But I'll make use of it.
